{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bcf503b421b546dfb7ccdc65","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Invalid Type Conversion Tests\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This test suite covers fundamentally invalid type conversions that should return\n4\t//! errors rather than panicking. All tests use the table-driven pattern for consistency\n5\t//! and maintainability.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Test Categories\n8\t//!\n9\t//! 1. **String to Non-String Conversions** - Invalid conversions from strings to scalar types\n10\t//! 2. **Struct to Scalar Conversions** - Invalid conversions from mappings/sequences to scalars\n11\t//! 3. **Array/Map to Invalid Scalar Conversions** - Invalid conversions from collections to primitives\n12\t//! 4. **Expected Integer But Got Boolean** - Boolean values where integers are expected\n13\t//! 5. **Expected String But Got Number** - Numeric values where strings are expected\n14\t//! 6. **Expected Array/Map But Got Scalar** - Scalar values where collections are expected\n15\t//! 7. **Edge Cases: Compatible But Wrong Types** - Integer/float cross-conversion, truthy values, etc.\n16\t//!\n17\t//! # Expected Behavior\n18\t//!\n19\t//! All invalid conversions must return `ParseError::type_mismatch()` and never panic.\n20\t\n21\tuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n22\tuse serde_yaml::Value;\n23\t\n24\t// ============================================================================\n25\t// String to Non-String Type Conversions\n26\t// ============================================================================\n27\t\n28\t#[test]\n29\tfn test_string_to_invalid_integer_conversions() {\n30\t    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to integers\n31\t    ///\n32\t    /// These test cases verify that non-numeric strings are properly rejected\n33\t    /// when attempting to convert them to integer types.\n34\t    ///\n35\t    /// # Test Cases\n36\t    ///\n37\t    /// | Input String | Description |\n38\t    /// |--------------|-------------|\n39\t    /// | \"abc\" | Pure alphabetic string |\n40\t    /// | \"12.34\" | Float-formatted string |\n41\t    /// | \"true\" | Boolean string |\n42\t    /// | \"null\" | Null string |\n43\t    /// | \"123abc\" | Mixed alphanumeric |\n44\t    /// | \"\" | Empty string |\n45\t    /// | \"inf\" | Infinity string |\n46\t    /// | \"-inf\" | Negative infinity |\n47\t\n48\t    let test_cases = vec![\n49\t        (\"abc\", \"pure alphabetic\"),\n50\t        (\"12.34\", \"float-formatted\"),\n51\t        (\"true\", \"boolean string\"),\n52\t        (\"null\", \"null string\"),\n53\t        (\"123abc\", \"mixed alphanumeric\"),\n54\t        (\"\", \"empty string\"),\n55\t        (\"inf\", \"infinity\"),\n56\t        (\"-inf\", \"negative infinity\"),\n57\t        (\"1e10\", \"scientific notation\"),\n58\t        (\"0xFF\", \"hexadecimal\"),\n59\t        (\"0o777\", \"octal\"),\n60\t    ];\n61\t\n62\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n63\t        let yaml = format!(\"port: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n64\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n65\t\n66\t        // Verify parsing succeeds (YAML accepts these as strings)\n67\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} input\", description);\n68\t\n69\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n70\t        let port_value = &value[\"port\"];\n71\t\n72\t        // Verify it's actually a string\n73\t        assert!(port_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n74\t\n75\t        // Verify conversion to integer fails\n76\t        let result = port_value.as_i64();\n77\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n78\t            \"String '{}' should not convert to integer ({})\",\n79\t            if input.is_empty() { \"<empty>\" } else { input },\n80\t            description\n81\t        );\n82\t\n83\t        // Verify we can create a type_mismatch error for this case\n84\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n85\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n86\t            \"Type mismatch error should be recognized for {}\", description);\n87\t\n88\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n89\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"port\"),\n90\t            \"Error message should include field name for {}\", description);\n91\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"integer\"),\n92\t            \"Error message should include expected type for {}\", description);\n93\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"string\"),\n94\t            \"Error message should include actual type for {}\", description);\n95\t    }\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\t#[test]\n99\tfn test_string_to_invalid_boolean_conversions() {\n100\t    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to booleans\n101\t    ///\n102\t    /// Only \"true\" and \"false\" (case-insensitive in some contexts) should\n103\t    /// convert to booleans. All other strings should fail.\n104\t    ///\n105\t    /// # Test Cases\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// | Input String | Description |\n108\t    /// |--------------|-------------|\n109\t    /// | \"yes\" | Affirmative but not boolean |\n110\t    /// | \"no\" | Negative but not boolean |\n111\t    /// | \"1\" | Numeric string |\n112\t    /// | \"0\" | Zero string |\n113\t    /// | \"on\" | State string |\n114\t    /// | \"off\" | Off state |\n115\t    /// | \"TRUE\" | Uppercase (may vary by parser) |\n116\t    /// | \"tRuE\" | Mixed case |\n117\t\n118\t    let test_cases = vec![\n119\t        (\"yes\", \"affirmative\"),\n120\t        (\"no\", \"negative\"),\n121\t        (\"1\", \"numeric one\"),\n122\t        (\"0\", \"numeric zero\"),\n123\t        (\"on\", \"on state\"),\n124\t        (\"off\", \"off state\"),\n125\t        (\"enabled\", \"enabled state\"),\n126\t        (\"disabled\", \"disabled state\"),\n127\t        (\"y\", \"single letter yes\"),\n128\t        (\"n\", \"single letter no\"),\n129\t    ];\n130\t\n131\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n132\t        let yaml = format!(\"enabled: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n133\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n134\t\n135\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n136\t\n137\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n138\t        let enabled_value = &value[\"enabled\"];\n139\t\n140\t        assert!(enabled_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n141\t\n142\t        // Verify conversion to boolean fails (for non-standard boolean strings)\n143\t        let _result = enabled_value.as_bool();\n144\t        // Note: Some YAML parsers may convert certain strings, so we check the error handling\n145\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"enabled\", \"boolean\", \"string\");\n146\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n147\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} string\", description);\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t#[test]\n152\tfn test_string_to_invalid_float_conversions() {\n153\t    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to floats\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// These test cases verify that non-numeric strings fail float conversion.\n156\t    ///\n157\t    /// # Test Cases\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// | Input String | Description |\n160\t    /// |--------------|-------------|\n161\t    /// | \"abc\" | Alphabetic |\n162\t    /// | \"true\" | Boolean string |\n163\t    /// | \"null\" | Null string |\n164\t\n165\t    let test_cases = vec![\n166\t        (\"abc\", \"alphabetic\"),\n167\t        (\"true\", \"boolean string\"),\n168\t        (\"null\", \"null string\"),\n169\t        (\"123abc\", \"mixed alphanumeric\"),\n170\t        (\"\", \"empty string\"),\n171\t        (\"..\", \"invalid float syntax\"),\n172\t        (\"1.2.3\", \"multiple dots\"),\n173\t    ];\n174\t\n175\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n176\t        let yaml = format!(\"rate: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n177\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n178\t\n179\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n180\t\n181\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n182\t        let rate_value = &value[\"rate\"];\n183\t\n184\t        assert!(rate_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n185\t\n186\t        // Verify conversion to float fails\n187\t        let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n188\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n189\t            \"String '{}' should not convert to float ({})\",\n190\t            if input.is_empty() { \"<empty>\" } else { input },\n191\t            description\n192\t        );\n193\t\n194\t        // Verify type mismatch error handling\n195\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"string\");\n196\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n197\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\", description);\n198\t    }\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// ============================================================================\n202\t// Struct to Scalar Conversion Failures\n203\t// ============================================================================\n204\t\n205\t#[test]\n206\tfn test_mapping_to_scalar_conversions() {\n207\t    /// Test: Mapping (object) to scalar conversions should fail\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// These test cases verify that mappings/objects cannot be converted to\n210\t    /// scalar primitive types (integer, boolean, float, string).\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// # Test Cases\n213\t    ///\n214\t    /// | Target Type | Description |\n215\t    /// |-------------|-------------|\n216\t    /// | integer | Mapping cannot become integer |\n217\t    /// | boolean | Mapping cannot become boolean |\n218\t    /// | float | Mapping cannot become float |\n219\t    /// | string | Mapping cannot become string (directly) |\n220\t    /// | null | Mapping cannot become null |\n221\t\n222\t    let test_cases = vec![\n223\t        (\"integer\", \"i64\", \"number\"),\n224\t        (\"boolean\", \"bool\", \"true/false\"),\n225\t        (\"float\", \"f64\", \"decimal\"),\n226\t        (\"string\", \"string\", \"text\"),\n227\t        (\"number\", \"number\", \"numeric\"),\n228\t    ];\n229\t\n230\t    let yaml_mapping = r#\"\n231\tconfig:\n232\t  host: localhost\n233\t  port: 8080\n234\t  timeout: 30\n235\t\"#;\n236\t\n237\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_mapping).unwrap();\n238\t    let config_value = &value[\"config\"];\n239\t\n240\t    assert!(config_value.is_mapping(), \"Config should be a mapping\");\n241\t\n242\t    for (field, expected_type, description) in test_cases {\n243\t        // Verify mapping cannot convert to scalar types\n244\t        let result = match expected_type {\n245\t            \"i64\" => config_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n246\t            \"bool\" => config_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n247\t            \"f64\" => config_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n248\t            \"string\" => config_value.as_str().is_some(),\n249\t            \"number\" => config_value.is_number(),\n250\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n251\t        };\n252\t\n253\t        assert!(!result,\n254\t            \"Mapping should not convert to {} ({})\", expected_type, description);\n255\t\n256\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n257\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"config\", expected_type, \"mapping\");\n258\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n259\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for mapping to {} conversion\", expected_type);\n260\t\n261\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n262\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"mapping\") || error_msg.contains(\"object\"),\n263\t            \"Error should mention actual type is mapping/object for {}\", description);\n264\t    }\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t#[test]\n268\tfn test_sequence_to_scalar_conversions() {\n269\t    /// Test: Sequence (array) to scalar conversions should fail\n270\t    ///\n271\t    /// These test cases verify that sequences/arrays cannot be converted to\n272\t    /// scalar primitive types.\n273\t    ///\n274\t    /// # Test Cases\n275\t    ///\n276\t    /// | Target Type | Description |\n277\t    /// |-------------|-------------|\n278\t    /// | integer | Array cannot become integer |\n279\t    /// | boolean | Array cannot become boolean |\n280\t    /// | float | Array cannot become float |\n281\t    /// | string | Array cannot become string (directly) |\n282\t    /// | number | Array cannot become number |\n283\t\n284\t    let test_cases = vec![\n285\t        (\"integer\", \"i64\"),\n286\t        (\"boolean\", \"bool\"),\n287\t        (\"float\", \"f64\"),\n288\t        (\"string\", \"str\"),\n289\t        (\"number\", \"number\"),\n290\t    ];\n291\t\n292\t    let yaml_sequence = r#\"\n293\tservers:\n294\t  - host: server1\n295\t    port: 8000\n296\t  - host: server2\n297\t    port: 8001\n298\t\"#;\n299\t\n300\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_sequence).unwrap();\n301\t    let servers_value = &value[\"servers\"];\n302\t\n303\t    assert!(servers_value.is_sequence(), \"Servers should be a sequence\");\n304\t\n305\t    for (field, expected_type) in test_cases {\n306\t        // Verify sequence cannot convert to scalar types\n307\t        let result = match expected_type {\n308\t            \"i64\" => servers_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n309\t            \"bool\" => servers_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n310\t            \"f64\" => servers_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n311\t            \"str\" => servers_value.as_str().is_some(),\n312\t            \"number\" => servers_value.is_number(),\n313\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n314\t        };\n315\t\n316\t        assert!(!result,\n317\t            \"Sequence should not convert to {}\", expected_type);\n318\t\n319\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n320\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"servers\", expected_type, \"sequence\");\n321\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n322\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for sequence to {} conversion\", expected_type);\n323\t\n324\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n325\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"sequence\") || error_msg.contains(\"array\"),\n326\t            \"Error should mention actual type is sequence/array for {}\", expected_type);\n327\t    }\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// ============================================================================\n331\t// Array/Map to Invalid Scalar Conversions\n332\t// ============================================================================\n333\t\n334\t#[test]\n335\tfn test_array_to_scalar_invalid_conversions() {\n336\t    /// Test: Array to scalar conversions that should always fail\n337\t    ///\n338\t    /// Comprehensive testing of array-to-scalar conversion failures.\n339\t    ///\n340\t    /// # Test Coverage\n341\t    ///\n342\t    /// - Empty array to scalar\n343\t    /// - Single-element array to scalar (still invalid - must explicitly access)\n344\t    /// - Multi-element array to scalar\n345\t    /// - Nested array to scalar\n346\t\n347\t    let test_cases = vec![\n348\t        (r#\"items: []\"#, \"empty array\", \"integer\"),\n349\t        (r#\"count: [42]\"#, \"single-element array\", \"integer\"),\n350\t        (r#\"ports: [8000, 8001, 8002]\"#, \"multi-element array\", \"integer\"),\n351\t        (r#\"matrix: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]\"#, \"nested array\", \"integer\"),\n352\t        (r#\"flags: [true, false]\"#, \"boolean array\", \"boolean\"),\n353\t        (r#\"rates: [1.5, 2.5, 3.5]\"#, \"float array\", \"float\"),\n354\t    ];\n355\t\n356\t    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n357\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n358\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n359\t\n360\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n361\t        // Get the first key-value pair\n362\t        let key = value.as_mapping().unwrap().keys().next().unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n363\t        let array_value = &value[key];\n364\t\n365\t        assert!(array_value.is_sequence(), \"Value should be array for {}\", description);\n366\t\n367\t        // Verify conversion fails\n368\t        let result = match expected_type {\n369\t            \"integer\" => array_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n370\t            \"boolean\" => array_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n371\t            \"float\" => array_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n372\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n373\t        };\n374\t\n375\t        assert!(!result,\n376\t            \"Array should not convert to {} for {}\", expected_type, description);\n377\t\n378\t        // Verify error handling\n379\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(key, expected_type, \"array\");\n380\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n381\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for array to {} ({})\", expected_type, description);\n382\t    }\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t#[test]\n386\tfn test_map_to_scalar_invalid_conversions() {\n387\t    /// Test: Map (object) to scalar conversions that should always fail\n388\t    ///\n389\t    /// Comprehensive testing of map-to-scalar conversion failures.\n390\t    ///\n391\t    /// # Test Coverage\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// - Empty map to scalar\n394\t    /// - Single-key map to scalar\n395\t    /// - Multi-key map to scalar\n396\t    /// - Nested map to scalar\n397\t\n398\t    let test_cases = vec![\n399\t        (r#\"config: {}\"#, \"empty map\", \"integer\"),\n400\t        (r#\"port: {number: 8080}\"#, \"single-key map\", \"integer\"),\n401\t        (r#\"server: {host: localhost, port: 8080}\"#, \"multi-key map\", \"string\"),\n402\t        (r#\"database: {host: localhost, port: 5432, name: test}\"#, \"multi-field map\", \"boolean\"),\n403\t        (r#\"nested: {outer: {inner: 42}}\"#, \"nested map\", \"integer\"),\n404\t    ];\n405\t\n406\t    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n407\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n408\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n409\t\n410\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n411\t        let key = value.as_mapping().unwrap().keys().next().unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n412\t        let map_value = &value[key];\n413\t\n414\t        assert!(map_value.is_mapping(), \"Value should be map for {}\", description);\n415\t\n416\t        // Verify conversion fails\n417\t        let result = match expected_type {\n418\t            \"integer\" => map_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n419\t            \"string\" => map_value.as_str().is_some(),\n420\t            \"boolean\" => map_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n421\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n422\t        };\n423\t\n424\t        assert!(!result,\n425\t            \"Map should not convert to {} for {}\", expected_type, description);\n426\t\n427\t        // Verify error handling\n428\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(key, expected_type, \"map\");\n429\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n430\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for map to {} ({})\", expected_type, description);\n431\t    }\n432\t}\n433\t\n434\t#[test]\n435\tfn test_array_to_map_invalid_conversion() {\n436\t    /// Test: Array to map conversion should fail\n437\t    ///\n438\t    /// Arrays cannot be treated as maps/objects - this is a structural mismatch.\n439\t\n440\t    let yaml_array = r#\"\n441\tservers:\n442\t  - name: server1\n443\t    port: 8000\n444\t  - name: server2\n445\t    port: 8001\n446\t\"#;\n447\t\n448\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_array).unwrap();\n449\t    let servers_value = &value[\"servers\"];\n450\t\n451\t    assert!(servers_value.is_sequence(), \"Servers should be an array\");\n452\t\n453\t    // Verify array cannot be treated as mapping\n454\t    assert!(servers_value.as_mapping().is_none(),\n455\t        \"Array should not convert to mapping\");\n456\t\n457\t    // Verify error handling\n458\t    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"servers\", \"map\", \"array\");\n459\t    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n460\t        \"Type mismatch error should be created for array to map conversion\");\n461\t\n462\t    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n463\t    assert!(error_msg.contains(\"array\"),\n464\t        \"Error should mention actual type is array\");\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t#[test]\n468\tfn test_map_to_array_invalid_conversion() {\n469\t    /// Test: Map to array conversion should fail\n470\t    ///\n471\t    /// Maps/objects cannot be treated as arrays - this is a structural mismatch.\n472\t\n473\t    let yaml_map = r#\"\n474\tserver:\n475\t  name: web\n476\t  port: 8080\n477\t  enabled: true\n478\t\"#;\n479\t\n480\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_map).unwrap();\n481\t    let server_value = &value[\"server\"];\n482\t\n483\t    assert!(server_value.is_mapping(), \"Server should be a map\");\n484\t\n485\t    // Verify map cannot be treated as sequence\n486\t    assert!(server_value.as_sequence().is_none(),\n487\t        \"Map should not convert to sequence\");\n488\t\n489\t    // Verify error handling\n490\t    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"server\", \"array\", \"map\");\n491\t    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n492\t        \"Type mismatch error should be created for map to array conversion\");\n493\t\n494\t    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n495\t    assert!(error_msg.contains(\"map\") || error_msg.contains(\"object\"),\n496\t        \"Error should mention actual type is map/object\");\n497\t}\n498\t\n499\t// ============================================================================\n500\t// Null Value Type Conversions\n501\t// ============================================================================\n502\t\n503\t#[test]\n504\tfn test_null_to_typed_scalar_conversions() {\n505\t    /// Test: Null to typed scalar conversions should fail\n506\t    ///\n507\t    /// Null values cannot be converted to non-nullable scalar types.\n508\t    ///\n509\t    /// # Test Cases\n510\t    ///\n511\t    /// | Target Type | Description |\n512\t    /// |-------------|-------------|\n513\t    /// | integer | Null is not an integer |\n514\t    /// | boolean | Null is not a boolean |\n515\t    /// | float | Null is not a float |\n516\t    /// | string | Null is not a string |\n517\t\n518\t    let yaml = r#\"\n519\tport: ~\n520\tenabled: null\n521\trate: null\n522\tname: null\n523\t\"#;\n524\t\n525\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n526\t\n527\t    let test_cases = vec![\n528\t        (\"port\", \"integer\"),\n529\t        (\"enabled\", \"boolean\"),\n530\t        (\"rate\", \"float\"),\n531\t        (\"name\", \"string\"),\n532\t    ];\n533\t\n534\t    for (field, expected_type) in test_cases {\n535\t        let field_value = &value[field];\n536\t\n537\t        assert!(field_value.is_null(), \"{} should be null\", field);\n538\t\n539\t        // Verify null cannot convert to typed scalar\n540\t        let result = match expected_type {\n541\t            \"integer\" => field_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n542\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n543\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n544\t            \"string\" => field_value.as_str().is_some(),\n545\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n546\t        };\n547\t\n548\t        assert!(!result,\n549\t            \"Null should not convert to {} for field {}\", expected_type, field);\n550\t\n551\t        // Verify error handling\n552\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field, expected_type, \"null\");\n553\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n554\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for null to {} conversion\", expected_type);\n555\t    }\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\t// ============================================================================\n559\t// Complex Nested Type Conversions\n560\t// ============================================================================\n561\t\n562\t#[test]\n563\tfn test_nested_field_type_mismatch() {\n564\t    /// Test: Type mismatches in deeply nested fields\n565\t    ///\n566\t    /// These test cases verify type conversion errors in nested structures.\n567\t    ///\n568\t    /// # Test Cases\n569\t    ///\n570\t    /// | Field Path | Expected | Actual | Description |\n571\t    /// |------------|----------|--------|-------------|\n572\t    /// | database.port | integer | string | Port is string, not integer |\n573\t    /// | server.enabled | boolean | integer | Enabled is number, not boolean |\n574\t    /// | config.rate | float | boolean | Rate is boolean, not float |\n575\t\n576\t    let yaml = r#\"\n577\tdatabase:\n578\t  host: localhost\n579\t  port: \"5432\"\n580\t  name: test\n581\tserver:\n582\t  host: localhost\n583\t  port: 8080\n584\t  enabled: 1\n585\tconfig:\n586\t  rate: true\n587\t  timeout: 30\n588\t\"#;\n589\t\n590\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n591\t\n592\t    let test_cases = vec![\n593\t        (\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n594\t        (\"server.enabled\", \"boolean\", \"integer\"),\n595\t        (\"config.rate\", \"float\", \"boolean\"),\n596\t    ];\n597\t\n598\t    for (field_path, expected, actual) in test_cases {\n599\t        // Navigate to nested field\n600\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = field_path.split('.').collect();\n601\t        let mut current = &value;\n602\t\n603\t        for part in &parts {\n604\t            current = &current[*part];\n605\t        }\n606\t\n607\t        // Verify actual type matches expectation\n608\t        let is_actual_type = match actual {\n609\t            \"string\" => current.is_string(),\n610\t            \"integer\" => current.is_i64(),\n611\t            \"boolean\" => current.is_bool(),\n612\t            _ => false,\n613\t        };\n614\t\n615\t        assert!(is_actual_type,\n616\t            \"Field {} should be {} type\", field_path, actual);\n617\t\n618\t        // Verify conversion to expected type fails\n619\t        let conversion_succeeds = match expected {\n620\t            \"integer\" => current.as_i64().is_some(),\n621\t            \"boolean\" => current.as_bool().is_some(),\n622\t            \"float\" => current.as_f64().is_some(),\n623\t            \"string\" => current.as_str().is_some(),\n624\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected),\n625\t        };\n626\t\n627\t        assert!(!conversion_succeeds,\n628\t            \"Field {} ({}) should not convert to {}\", field_path, actual, expected);\n629\t\n630\t        // Verify error handling with proper field path\n631\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_path, expected, actual);\n632\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n633\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} field path\", field_path);\n634\t\n635\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n636\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(field_path),\n637\t            \"Error should include field path '{}'\", field_path);\n638\t    }\n639\t}\n640\t\n641\t// ============================================================================\n642\t// Type Conversion Error Consistency\n643\t// ============================================================================\n644\t\n645\t#[test]\n646\tfn test_type_mismatch_error_formatting() {\n647\t    /// Test: Type mismatch error formatting consistency\n648\t    ///\n649\t    /// Verify that all type mismatch errors produce consistent, well-formatted messages.\n650\t\n651\t    let test_cases = vec![\n652\t        (\"field1\", \"integer\", \"string\", \"Integer field got string\"),\n653\t        (\"timeout\", \"integer\", \"boolean\", \"Timeout is boolean, not integer\"),\n654\t        (\"enabled\", \"boolean\", \"integer\", \"Enabled is integer, not boolean\"),\n655\t        (\"rate\", \"float\", \"string\", \"Rate is string, not float\"),\n656\t        (\"hosts\", \"array\", \"string\", \"Hosts should be array, got string\"),\n657\t        (\"config\", \"object\", \"null\", \"Config is null, not object\"),\n658\t        (\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"string\", \"Nested integer field got string\"),\n659\t    ];\n660\t\n661\t    for (field, expected, actual, description) in test_cases {\n662\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field, expected, actual);\n663\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n664\t\n665\t        // Verify error message contains all components\n666\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(field),\n667\t            \"Error should contain field name for: {}\", description);\n668\t\n669\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(expected),\n670\t            \"Error should contain expected type for: {}\", description);\n671\t\n672\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(actual),\n673\t            \"Error should contain actual type for: {}\", description);\n674\t\n675\t        // Verify error is correctly categorized\n676\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n677\t            \"Error should be type mismatch category for: {}\", description);\n678\t\n679\t        // Verify error is not other categories\n680\t        assert!(!error.is_syntax(),\n681\t            \"Type mismatch should not be syntax error for: {}\", description);\n682\t        assert!(!error.is_validation(),\n683\t            \"Type mismatch should not be validation error for: {}\", description);\n684\t    }\n685\t}\n686\t\n687\t#[test]\n688\tfn test_type_mismatch_no_panic_on_invalid_conversion() {\n689\t    /// Test: Invalid conversions must not panic\n690\t    ///\n691\t    /// This is a critical safety test - no invalid type conversion should ever panic.\n692\t    /// All conversions must return None or Result::Err.\n693\t\n694\t    let yaml = r#\"\n695\tstring_field: \"not a number\"\n696\tbool_field: \"not a boolean\"\n697\tarray_field: [1, 2, 3]\n698\tmap_field: {key: value}\n699\tnull_field: null\n700\t\"#;\n701\t\n702\t    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).unwrap();\n703\t\n704\t    // Test all combinations of invalid conversions - none should panic\n705\t    // Note: Some conversions succeed in serde_yaml (e.g., bool->str, int->str, float->str)\n706\t    // so we only test the truly invalid conversions\n707\t    let test_cases = vec![\n708\t        (&value[\"string_field\"], \"string_field\", \"string\", vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_f64\"]),\n709\t        (&value[\"bool_field\"], \"bool_field\", \"boolean\", vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_f64\"]),\n710\t        (&value[\"array_field\"], \"array_field\", \"array\", vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_str\", \"as_f64\", \"as_mapping\"]),\n711\t        (&value[\"map_field\"], \"map_field\", \"map\", vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_str\", \"as_f64\", \"as_sequence\"]),\n712\t        (&value[\"null_field\"], \"null_field\", \"null\", vec![\"as_i64\", \"as_bool\", \"as_f64\"]),\n713\t    ];\n714\t\n715\t    for (value_ref, field_name, actual_type, methods) in test_cases {\n716\t        for method in methods {\n717\t            // Call conversion method - must not panic\n718\t            let conversion_result: bool = match method {\n719\t                \"as_i64\" => value_ref.as_i64().is_some(),\n720\t                \"as_bool\" => value_ref.as_bool().is_some(),\n721\t                \"as_str\" => value_ref.as_str().is_some(),\n722\t                \"as_f64\" => value_ref.as_f64().is_some(),\n723\t                \"as_mapping\" => value_ref.as_mapping().is_some(),\n724\t                \"as_sequence\" => value_ref.as_sequence().is_some(),\n725\t                _ => continue,\n726\t            };\n727\t\n728\t            // Verify conversion failed safely\n729\t            assert!(!conversion_result,\n730\t                \"Conversion {} for {} ({}) should fail, got success\",\n731\t                method, field_name, actual_type);\n732\t\n733\t            // Verify we can create an error for this case\n734\t            let expected_type: &str = match method {\n735\t                \"as_i64\" => \"integer\",\n736\t                \"as_bool\" => \"boolean\",\n737\t                \"as_str\" => \"string\",\n738\t                \"as_f64\" => \"float\",\n739\t                \"as_mapping\" => \"map\",\n740\t                \"as_sequence\" => \"array\",\n741\t                _ => continue,\n742\t            };\n743\t\n744\t            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, expected_type, actual_type);\n745\t            assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n746\t                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} -> {} conversion ({})\",\n747\t                actual_type, expected_type, method);\n748\t        }\n749\t    }\n750\t}\n751\t\n752\t// ============================================================================\n753\t// Type Mismatch: Expected Integer But Got Boolean\n754\t// ============================================================================\n755\t\n756\t#[test]\n757\tfn test_expected_integer_got_boolean() {\n758\t    /// Test: Integer fields receiving boolean values\n759\t    ///\n760\t    /// These test cases verify that boolean values are properly rejected when\n761\t    /// integers are expected. This is a common error in YAML configurations.\n762\t    ///\n763\t    /// # Test Cases\n764\t    ///\n765\t    /// | Field | Input | Description |\n766\t    /// |-------|-------|-------------|\n767\t    /// | port | true | Boolean true where integer needed |\n768\t    /// | port | false | Boolean false where integer needed |\n769\t    /// | count | true | Boolean true as count |\n770\t    /// | timeout | false | Boolean false as timeout |\n771\t    /// | size | true | Boolean true as size |\n772\t\n773\t    let test_cases = vec![\n774\t        (r#\"port: true\"#, \"port\", \"true\"),\n775\t        (r#\"port: false\"#, \"port\", \"false\"),\n776\t        (r#\"count: true\"#, \"count\", \"true\"),\n777\t        (r#\"timeout: false\"#, \"timeout\", \"false\"),\n778\t        (r#\"size: true\"#, \"size\", \"true\"),\n779\t        (r#\"limit: false\"#, \"limit\", \"false\"),\n780\t    ];\n781\t\n782\t    for (yaml, field_name, boolean_value) in test_cases {\n783\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n784\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for boolean {}\", boolean_value);\n785\t\n786\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n787\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n788\t\n789\t        // Verify it's actually a boolean\n790\t        assert!(field_value.is_bool(), \"Field {} should be boolean ({})\", field_name, boolean_value);\n791\t\n792\t        // Verify conversion to integer fails\n793\t        let result = field_value.as_i64();\n794\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n795\t            \"Boolean {} should not convert to integer for field {}\",\n796\t            boolean_value, field_name);\n797\t\n798\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n799\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"integer\", \"boolean\");\n800\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n801\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for boolean {} in integer field {}\",\n802\t            boolean_value, field_name);\n803\t\n804\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n805\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"integer\"),\n806\t            \"Error should mention expected integer type for {}\", field_name);\n807\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"boolean\") || error_msg.contains(\"bool\"),\n808\t            \"Error should mention actual boolean type for {}\", field_name);\n809\t    }\n810\t}\n811\t\n812\t// ============================================================================\n813\t// Type Mismatch: Expected String But Got Number\n814\t// ============================================================================\n815\t\n816\t#[test]\n817\tfn test_expected_string_got_number() {\n818\t    /// Test: String fields receiving numeric values\n819\t    ///\n820\t    /// These test cases verify that numeric values (integers and floats) are\n821\t    /// properly rejected when strings are expected.\n822\t    ///\n823\t    /// # Test Cases\n824\t    ///\n825\t    /// | Field | Input | Description |\n826\t    /// |-------|-------|-------------|\n827\t    /// | name | 123 | Integer where string needed |\n828\t    /// | hostname | 8080 | Integer port as hostname |\n829\t    /// | label | 3.14 | Float where string needed |\n830\t    /// | description | 42 | Integer as description |\n831\t    /// | path | 999 | Integer where path string needed |\n832\t\n833\t    let test_cases = vec![\n834\t        (r#\"name: 123\"#, \"name\", \"123\", \"integer\"),\n835\t        (r#\"hostname: 8080\"#, \"hostname\", \"8080\", \"integer\"),\n836\t        (r#\"label: 3.14\"#, \"label\", \"3.14\", \"float\"),\n837\t        (r#\"description: 42\"#, \"description\", \"42\", \"integer\"),\n838\t        (r#\"path: 999\"#, \"path\", \"999\", \"integer\"),\n839\t        (r#\"version: 1.0\"#, \"version\", \"1.0\", \"float\"),\n840\t        (r#\"id: 0\"#, \"id\", \"0\", \"integer\"),\n841\t        (r#\"title: 100\"#, \"title\", \"100\", \"integer\"),\n842\t    ];\n843\t\n844\t    for (yaml, field_name, numeric_value, value_type) in test_cases {\n845\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n846\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for numeric {}\", numeric_value);\n847\t\n848\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n849\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n850\t\n851\t        // Verify it's actually a number (not a string)\n852\t        assert!(!field_value.is_string(),\n853\t            \"Field {} should not be string (got numeric {})\", field_name, numeric_value);\n854\t\n855\t        // Verify it's a number\n856\t        let is_number = if value_type == \"integer\" {\n857\t            field_value.is_i64()\n858\t        } else {\n859\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n860\t        };\n861\t        assert!(is_number,\n862\t            \"Field {} should be {} type (value: {})\",\n863\t            field_name, value_type, numeric_value);\n864\t\n865\t        // Verify conversion to string fails\n866\t        let result = field_value.as_str();\n867\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n868\t            \"Numeric {} should not convert to string for field {}\",\n869\t            numeric_value, field_name);\n870\t\n871\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n872\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"string\", value_type);\n873\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n874\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} {} in string field {}\",\n875\t            value_type, numeric_value, field_name);\n876\t\n877\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n878\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"string\"),\n879\t            \"Error should mention expected string type for {}\", field_name);\n880\t    }\n881\t}\n882\t\n883\t// ============================================================================\n884\t// Type Mismatch: Expected Array/Slice But Got Scalar\n885\t// ============================================================================\n886\t\n887\t#[test]\n888\tfn test_expected_array_got_scalar() {\n889\t    /// Test: Array fields receiving scalar values\n890\t    ///\n891\t    /// These test cases verify that scalar values (integers, strings, booleans)\n892\t    /// are properly rejected when arrays/slices are expected.\n893\t    ///\n894\t    /// # Test Cases\n895\t    ///\n896\t    /// | Expected Type | Actual Input | Description |\n897\t    /// |---------------|--------------|-------------|\n898\t    /// | array | 42 | Integer where array needed |\n899\t    /// | array | \"single\" | String where array needed |\n900\t    /// | array | true | Boolean where array needed |\n901\t    /// | array | 3.14 | Float where array needed |\n902\t    /// | array | null | Null where array needed |\n903\t\n904\t    let test_cases = vec![\n905\t        (r#\"servers: 42\"#, \"servers\", \"integer\", \"42\"),\n906\t        (r#\"hosts: \"single\"\"#, \"hosts\", \"string\", \"single\"),\n907\t        (r#\"ports: true\"#, \"ports\", \"boolean\", \"true\"),\n908\t        (r#\"rates: 3.14\"#, \"rates\", \"float\", \"3.14\"),\n909\t        (r#\"items: null\"#, \"items\", \"null\", \"null\"),\n910\t        (r#\"tags: 0\"#, \"tags\", \"integer\", \"0\"),\n911\t        (r#\"keys: 1\"#, \"keys\", \"integer\", \"1\"),\n912\t        (r#\"values: false\"#, \"values\", \"boolean\", \"false\"),\n913\t    ];\n914\t\n915\t    for (yaml, field_name, actual_type, actual_value) in test_cases {\n916\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n917\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} {}\", actual_type, actual_value);\n918\t\n919\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n920\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n921\t\n922\t        // Verify it's not a sequence/array\n923\t        assert!(!field_value.is_sequence(),\n924\t            \"Field {} should not be sequence (got {} {})\",\n925\t            field_name, actual_type, actual_value);\n926\t\n927\t        // Verify it matches the expected actual type\n928\t        let matches_actual_type = match actual_type {\n929\t            \"integer\" => field_value.is_i64(),\n930\t            \"string\" => field_value.is_string(),\n931\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.is_bool(),\n932\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n933\t            \"null\" => field_value.is_null(),\n934\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected actual type: {}\", actual_type),\n935\t        };\n936\t        assert!(matches_actual_type,\n937\t            \"Field {} should be {} type (value: {})\",\n938\t            field_name, actual_type, actual_value);\n939\t\n940\t        // Verify conversion to sequence fails\n941\t        let result = field_value.as_sequence();\n942\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n943\t            \"{} {} should not convert to sequence for field {}\",\n944\t            actual_type, actual_value, field_name);\n945\t\n946\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n947\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"array\", actual_type);\n948\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n949\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} {} in array field {}\",\n950\t            actual_type, actual_value, field_name);\n951\t\n952\t        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n953\t        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"array\") || error_msg.contains(\"sequence\"),\n954\t            \"Error should mention expected array/sequence type for {}\", field_name);\n955\t    }\n956\t}\n957\t\n958\t// ============================================================================\n959\t// Type Mismatch: Expected Map But Got Scalar\n960\t// values\n965\t    ///\n966\t    /// These test cases verify that scalar values are properly rejected when\n967\t    /// maps/objects are expected.\n968\t    ///\n969\t    /// # Test Cases\n970\t \"Field {} should not be mapping (got {})\", field_name, actual_type);\n996\t\n997\t        // Verify conversion to mapping fails\n998\t        let result = field_value.as_mapping();\n999\t        assert!(result.is_none(),\n1000\t            \"{} should not convert to mapping for field {}\", actual_type, field_name);\n1001\t\n1002\t        // Verify type mismatch error is properly ============================================================================\n1015\t// Edge Cases: Compatible But Wrong Types\n1016\t// ============================================================================\n1017\t\n1018\t#[test]\n1019\tfn test_integer_float_cross_conversion() {\n1020\t    /// Test: Integer vs Float cross-conversion issues\n1021\t    ///\n1022\t    /// These test cases verify that integers and floats are distinguished\n1023\t    /// even when they may appear compatible.\n1024\t    ///\n1025\t    /// # Test Cases\n1026\t    ///\n1027\t    /// | Expected | Actual | Input | Description |\n1028\t    /// |----------|--------|-------|-------------|\n1029\t    /// | integer | float | 3.14 | Float where integer needed |\n1030\t    /// | float | integer | 42 | Integer where float needed |\n1031\t    /// | integer | float | 1.0 | Whole number as float |\n1032\t    /// | float | integer | 0 | Zero as integer |\n1033\t\n1034\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1035\t        (r#\"port: 3.14\"#, \"port\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1036\t        (r#\"rate: 42\"#, \"rate\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1037\t        (r#\"count: 1.0\"#, \"count\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1038\t        (r#\"timeout: 0\"#, \"timeout\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1039\t        (r#\"size: 2.5\"#, \"size\", \"integer\", \"float\"),\n1040\t        (r#\"ratio: 100\"#, \"ratio\", \"float\", \"integer\"),\n1041\t    ];\n1042\t\n1043\t    for (yaml, field_name, expected_type, actual_type) in test_cases {\n1044\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n1045\t        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} to {}\", actual_type, expected_type);\n1046\t\n1047\t        let value = value.unwrap();\n1048\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1049\t\n1050\t        // Verify actual type\n1051\t        let is_actual_type = if actual_type == \"integer\" {\n1052\t            field_value.is_i64()\n1053\t        } else {\n1054\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n1055\t        };\n1056\t        assert!(is_actual_type,\n1057\t            \"Field {} should be {} type\", field_name, actual_type);\n1058\t\n1059\t        // Verify conversion to expected type fails\n1060\t        let conversion_succeeds = if expected_type == \"integer\" {\n1061\t            field_value.as_i64().is_some()\n1062\t        } else {\n1063\t            field_value.as_f64().is_some()\n1064\t        };\n1065\t\n1066\t        // For float->integer conversion, we expect failure\n1067\t        // For integer->float conversion, we expect success in serde_yaml\n1068\t        // but should fail if strict type checking is required\n1069\t        if actual_type == \"float\" && expected_type == \"integer\" {\n1070\t            assert!(!conversion_succeeds || field_value.as_f64().unwrap().fract() != 0.0,\n1071\t                \"Float with fractional part should not convert to integer for {}\", field_name);\n1072\t        }\n1073\t\n1074\t        // Verify type mismatch error can be created\n1075\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, expected_type, actual_type);\n1076\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1077\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} to {} conversion\",\n1078\t            actual_type, expected_type);\n1079\t    }\n1080\t}\n1081\t\n1082\t#[test]\n1083\tfn test_truthy_values_as_booleans() {\n1084\t    /// Test: Truthy numeric values mistaken for booleans\n1085\t    ///\n1086\t    /// test_incompatible_scalar_to_scalar_conversions() {\n1239\t    /// Test: Incompatible scalar-to-scalar conversions\n1240\t    ///\n1241\t    /// These test cases verify various scalar type mismatches.\n1242\t    ///\n1243\t    /// # Test Cases\n1244\t    ///\n1245\t    /// | Expected | Actual | Description |\n1246\t    /// |----------|--------|-------------|\n1247\t    /// | integer | \"count\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n1258\t        (r#\"flag: 1\"#, \"flag\", \"boolean\", \"integer\"),\n1259\t        (r#\"timeout: \"fast\"\"#, \"timeout\", \"integer\", \"string\"),\n1260\t        (r#\"ratio: true\"#, \"ratio\", \"float\", \"boolean\"),\n1261\t    ];\n1262\t\n1263\t    for (yaml, field_name, expected_type, actual_type) in test_cases {\n1264\t        let value: Result<Value, _> value.unwrap();\n1269\t        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n1270\t\n1271\t        // Verify actual type matches\n1272\t        let is_actual_type = match actual_type {\n1273\t            \"boolean\" => field_value.is_bool(),\n1274\t            \"string\" => field_value.is_string(),\n1275\t            \"integer\" => field_value.is_i64(),\n1276\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n1277\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected actual type: {}\", actual_type),\n1278\t        };\n1279\t        assert!(is_actual_type,\n1280\t            \"Field {} \"boolean\" => field_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n1285\t            \"string\" => field_value.as_str().is_some(),\n1286\t            \"integer\" => field_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n1287\t            \"float\" => field_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n1288\t            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n1289\t        };\n1290\t\n1291\t        assert!(!conversion_succeeds,\n1292\t            \"{} should not convert to {} for field {}\",\n1293\t            actual_type, expected_type, field_name);\n1294\t\n1295\t        // Verify type \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} -> {} in field {}\",\n1299\t            actual_type, expected_type, field_name);\n1300\t    }\n1301\t}\n1302\t\n1303\t// ============================================================================\n1304\t// Integer Overflow/Underflow Tests\n1305\t// ============================================================================\n1306\t\n1307\t#[test]\n1308\tfn test_integer_overflow_values() {\n1309\t    /// Test: Integer overflow scenarios\n1310\t    ///\n1311\t    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding the maximum representable\n1312\t    /// integer values are properly handled and produce appropriate errors.\n1313\t    ///\n1314\t    /// # Test Cases\n1315\t    ///\n1316\t    /// | Input | Description |\n1317\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n1318\t    /// | 9223372036854775808 | i64::MAX + 1 (overflow) |\n1319\t    /// | 9999999999999999999 | Arbitrary large number |\n1320\t    /// | 18446744073709551615 | u64::MAX (overflow for i64) |\n1321\t    /// | 100000000000000000000 | Extremely large value |\n1322\t    /// | 92233720368547758070 | 10x i64::MAX |\n1323\t\n1324\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1325\t        (\"9223372036854775808\", \"i64_MAX + 1\"),\n1326\t        (\"9999999999999999999\", \"arbitrary large number\"),\n1327\t        (\"18446744073709551615\", \"u64_MAX\"),\n1328\t        (\"100000000000000000000\", \"extremely large value\"),\n1329\t        (\"92233720368547758070\", \"10x i64_MAX\"),\n1330\t    ];\n1331\t\n1332\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n1333\t        let yaml = format!(\"port: {}\", input);\n1334\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n1335\t\n1336\t        // YAML may parse these as strings or integers depending on implementation\n1337\t        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n1338\t            let port_value = &parsed_value[\"port\"];\n1339\t\n1340\t            // If parsed as integer, verify it overflows or is clamped\n1341\t            if port_value.is_i64() {\n1342\t                let int_value = port_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n1343\t                // If YAML clamped to max, that's acceptable behavior\n1344\t                assert!(int_value <= i64::MAX,\n1345\t                    \"Integer should be at most i64::MAX for {}\", description);\n1346\t            } else if port_value.is_string() {\n1347\t                // String representation - cannot convert to integer\n1348\t                let result = port_value.as_i64();\n1349\t                assert!(result.is_none(),\n1350\t                    \"Overflow string '{}' should not convert to integer ({})\",\n1351\t                    input, description);\n1352\t\n1353\t                // Verify type mismatch error handling\n1354\t                let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"overflow\");\n1355\t                assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1356\t                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow case {}\", description);\n1357\t            } else if port_value.is_u64() {\n1358\t                // Some YAML parsers use u64 for large values\n1359\t                let u64_value = port_value.as_u64().unwrap();\n1360\t                assert!(u64_value > i64::MAX as u64,\n1361\t                    \"u64 value should exceed i64::MAX for {}\", description);\n1362\t            }\n1363\t        }\n1364\t\n1365\t        // Verify error handling for overflow scenario\n1366\t        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"overflow\");\n1367\t        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1368\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow {}\", description);\n1369\t    }\n1370\t}\n1371\t\n1372\t#[test]\n1373\tfn test_integer_underflow_values() {\n1374\t    /// Test: Integer underflow scenarios\n1375\t    ///\n1376\t    /// These test cases verify that values below the minimum representable\n1377\t    /// integer values are properly handled.\n1378\t    ///\n1379\t    /// # Test Cases\n1380\t    ///\n1381\t    /// | Input | Description |\n1382\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n1383\t    /// | -9223372036854775809 | i64::MIN - 1 (underflow) |\n1384\t    /// | -9999999999999999999 | Arbitrary very negative number |\n1385\t    /// | -92233720368547758080 | 10x i64::MIN |\n1386\t    /// | -18446744073709551615 | Extremely negative |\n1387\t\n1388\t    let test_cases = vec![\n1389\t        (\"-9223372036854775809\", \"i64_MIN - 1\"),\n1390\t        (\"-9999999999999999999\", \"arbitrary very negative number\"),\n1391\t        (\"-92233720368547758080\", \"10x i64_MIN\"),\n1392\t        (\"-18446744073709551615\", \"extremely negative value\"),\n1393\t    ];\n1394\t\n1395\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n1396\t        let yaml = format!(\"port: {}\", input);\n1397\t        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n1398\t\n1399\t        // YAML may parse these as strings or handle underflow differently\n1400\t        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n1401\t            let port_value = &parsed_value[\"port\"];\n1402\t\n1403\t            if port_value.is_string() {\n1404\t                // String representation - cannot convert to integer\n1405\t                let result = port_value.as_i64();\n1406\t                assert!(result.is_none(),\n1407\t                    \"Underflow string '{}' should not convert to integer ({})\",\n1408\t                    input, description);\n1409\t\n1410\t                // Verify type mismatch error handling\n1411\t                let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"underflow\");\n1412\t                assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1413\t                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for underflow case {}\", description);\n1414\t            } else if port_value.is_i64() {\n1415\t                let int_value = port_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n1416\t                // If YAML clamped to assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1425\t            \"Type mismatch error should be created for underflow {}\", description);\n1426\t    }\n1427\t}\n1428\t\n1429\t#[test]\n1430\tfn properly fail when\n1434\t    /// converted to unsigned types (u8, u16, u32, u64).\n1435\t    ///\n1436\t    /// # Test Cases\n1437\t    ///\n1438\t    /// | Input | Target Type | Description Floating point overflow scenarios\n1788\t    ///\n1789\t    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding f64::MAX are properly handled.\n1790\t    ///\n1791\t    /// # Test Cases\n1792\t    ///\n1793\t    /// | Input | Description |\n1794\t    /// |-------|-------------|\n1795\t    /// | 1e400 | Value exceeding f64::MAX |\n1796\t    /// | 1e1000 | Extremely large value |\n1797\t    /// | (\"1e1000\", \"exponential 1000\"),\n1803\t        (\"1.7976931348623157e+309\", \"f64_MAX + 1\"),\n1804\t    ];\n1805\t\n1806\t    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n1807\t        let yaml = &parsed_value[\"rate\"];\n1812\t\n1813\t            if rate_value.is_string() {\n1814\t                // String representation - cannot convert to float\n1815\t                let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n1816\t                assert!(result.is_none(),\n1817\t                    \"Overflow string '{}' should not convert to float ({})\",\n1818\t                    input,    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n1823\t                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for float overflow {}\", description);\n1824\t            } else if rate_value.is_f64() Invalid Type Conversion Tests\n//!\n//! This test suite covers fundamentally invalid type conversions that should return\n//! errors rather than panicking. All tests use the table-driven pattern for consistency\n//! and maintainability.\n//!\n//! # Test Categories\n//!\n//! 1. **String to Non-String Conversions** - Invalid conversions from strings to scalar types\n//! 2. **Struct to Scalar Conversions** - Invalid conversions from mappings/sequences to scalars\n//! 3. **Array/Map to Invalid Scalar Conversions** - Invalid conversions from collections to primitives\n//! 4. **Expected Integer But Got Boolean** - Boolean values where integers are expected\n//! 5. **Expected String But Got Number** - Numeric values where strings are expected\n//! 6. **Expected Array/Map But Got Scalar** - Scalar values where collections are expected\n//! 7. **Edge Cases: Compatible But Wrong Types** - Integer/float cross-conversion, truthy values, etc.\n//!\n//! # Expected Behavior\n//!\n//! All invalid conversions must return `ParseError::type_mismatch()` and never panic.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\nuse serde_yaml::Value;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// String to Non-String Type Conversions\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_string_to_invalid_integer_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to integers\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that non-numeric strings are properly rejected\n    /// when attempting to convert them to integer types.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input String | Description |\n    /// |--------------|-------------|\n    /// | \"abc\" | Pure alphabetic string |\n    /// | \"12.34\" | Float-formatted string |\n    /// | \"true\" | Boolean string |\n    /// | \"null\" | Null string |\n    /// | \"123abc\" | Mixed alphanumeric |\n    /// | \"\" | Empty string |\n    /// | \"inf\" | Infinity string |\n    /// | \"-inf\" | Negative infinity |\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"abc\", \"pure alphabetic\"),\n        (\"12.34\", \"float-formatted\"),\n        (\"true\", \"boolean string\"),\n        (\"null\", \"null string\"),\n        (\"123abc\", \"mixed alphanumeric\"),\n        (\"\", \"empty string\"),\n        (\"inf\", \"infinity\"),\n        (\"-inf\", \"negative infinity\"),\n        (\"1e10\", \"scientific notation\"),\n        (\"0xFF\", \"hexadecimal\"),\n        (\"0o777\", \"octal\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"port: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        // Verify parsing succeeds (YAML accepts these as strings)\n        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} input\", description);\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let port_value = &value[\"port\"];\n\n        // Verify it's actually a string\n        assert!(port_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify conversion to integer fails\n        let result = port_value.as_i64();\n        assert!(result.is_none(),\n            \"String '{}' should not convert to integer ({})\",\n            if input.is_empty() { \"<empty>\" } else { input },\n            description\n        );\n\n        // Verify we can create a type_mismatch error for this case\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be recognized for {}\", description);\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"port\"),\n            \"Error message should include field name for {}\", description);\n        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"integer\"),\n            \"Error message should include expected type for {}\", description);\n        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"string\"),\n            \"Error message should include actual type for {}\", description);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_string_to_invalid_boolean_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to booleans\n    ///\n    /// Only \"true\" and \"false\" (case-insensitive in some contexts) should\n    /// convert to booleans. All other strings should fail.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input String | Description |\n    /// |--------------|-------------|\n    /// | \"yes\" | Affirmative but not boolean |\n    /// | \"no\" | Negative but not boolean |\n    /// | \"1\" | Numeric string |\n    /// | \"0\" | Zero string |\n    /// | \"on\" | State string |\n    /// | \"off\" | Off state |\n    /// | \"TRUE\" | Uppercase (may vary by parser) |\n    /// | \"tRuE\" | Mixed case |\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"yes\", \"affirmative\"),\n        (\"no\", \"negative\"),\n        (\"1\", \"numeric one\"),\n        (\"0\", \"numeric zero\"),\n        (\"on\", \"on state\"),\n        (\"off\", \"off state\"),\n        (\"enabled\", \"enabled state\"),\n        (\"disabled\", \"disabled state\"),\n        (\"y\", \"single letter yes\"),\n        (\"n\", \"single letter no\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"enabled: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let enabled_value = &value[\"enabled\"];\n\n        assert!(enabled_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify conversion to boolean fails (for non-standard boolean strings)\n        let _result = enabled_value.as_bool();\n        // Note: Some YAML parsers may convert certain strings, so we check the error handling\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"enabled\", \"boolean\", \"string\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} string\", description);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_string_to_invalid_float_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Strings that cannot be converted to floats\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that non-numeric strings fail float conversion.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input String | Description |\n    /// |--------------|-------------|\n    /// | \"abc\" | Alphabetic |\n    /// | \"true\" | Boolean string |\n    /// | \"null\" | Null string |\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"abc\", \"alphabetic\"),\n        (\"true\", \"boolean string\"),\n        (\"null\", \"null string\"),\n        (\"123abc\", \"mixed alphanumeric\"),\n        (\"\", \"empty string\"),\n        (\"..\", \"invalid float syntax\"),\n        (\"1.2.3\", \"multiple dots\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"rate: \\\"{}\\\"\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let rate_value = &value[\"rate\"];\n\n        assert!(rate_value.is_string(), \"Value should be string for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify conversion to float fails\n        let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n        assert!(result.is_none(),\n            \"String '{}' should not convert to float ({})\",\n            if input.is_empty() { \"<empty>\" } else { input },\n            description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error handling\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"string\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\", description);\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Struct to Scalar Conversion Failures\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_mapping_to_scalar_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Mapping (object) to scalar conversions should fail\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that mappings/objects cannot be converted to\n    /// scalar primitive types (integer, boolean, float, string).\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Target Type | Description |\n    /// |-------------|-------------|\n    /// | integer | Mapping cannot become integer |\n    /// | boolean | Mapping cannot become boolean |\n    /// | float | Mapping cannot become float |\n    /// | string | Mapping cannot become string (directly) |\n    /// | null | Mapping cannot become null |\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"integer\", \"i64\", \"number\"),\n        (\"boolean\", \"bool\", \"true/false\"),\n        (\"float\", \"f64\", \"decimal\"),\n        (\"string\", \"string\", \"text\"),\n        (\"number\", \"number\", \"numeric\"),\n    ];\n\n    let yaml_mapping = r#\"\nconfig:\n  host: localhost\n  port: 8080\n  timeout: 30\n\"#;\n\n    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_mapping).unwrap();\n    let config_value = &value[\"config\"];\n\n    assert!(config_value.is_mapping(), \"Config should be a mapping\");\n\n    for (field, expected_type, description) in test_cases {\n        // Verify mapping cannot convert to scalar types\n        let result = match expected_type {\n            \"i64\" => config_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n            \"bool\" => config_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n            \"f64\" => config_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n            \"string\" => config_value.as_str().is_some(),\n            \"number\" => config_value.is_number(),\n            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n        };\n\n        assert!(!result,\n            \"Mapping should not convert to {} ({})\", expected_type, description);\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"config\", expected_type, \"mapping\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for mapping to {} conversion\", expected_type);\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"mapping\") || error_msg.contains(\"object\"),\n            \"Error should mention actual type is mapping/object for {}\", description);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_to_scalar_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Sequence (array) to scalar conversions should fail\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that sequences/arrays cannot be converted to\n    /// scalar primitive types.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Target Type | Description |\n    /// |-------------|-------------|\n    /// | integer | Array cannot become integer |\n    /// | boolean | Array cannot become boolean |\n    /// | float | Array cannot become float |\n    /// | string | Array cannot become string (directly) |\n    /// | number | Array cannot become number |\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"integer\", \"i64\"),\n        (\"boolean\", \"bool\"),\n        (\"float\", \"f64\"),\n        (\"string\", \"str\"),\n        (\"number\", \"number\"),\n    ];\n\n    let yaml_sequence = r#\"\nservers:\n  - host: server1\n    port: 8000\n  - host: server2\n    port: 8001\n\"#;\n\n    let value: Value = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_sequence).unwrap();\n    let servers_value = &value[\"servers\"];\n\n    assert!(servers_value.is_sequence(), \"Servers should be a sequence\");\n\n    for (field, expected_type) in test_cases {\n        // Verify sequence cannot convert to scalar types\n        let result = match expected_type {\n            \"i64\" => servers_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n            \"bool\" => servers_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n            \"f64\" => servers_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n            \"str\" => servers_value.as_str().is_some(),\n            \"number\" => servers_value.is_number(),\n            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected type: {}\", expected_type),\n        };\n\n        assert!(!result,\n            \"Sequence should not convert to {}\", expected_type);\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"servers\", expected_type, \"sequence\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for sequence to {} conversion\", expected_type);\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(error_msg.contains(\"sequence\") || error_msg.contains(\"array\"),\n            \"Error should mention actual type is sequence/array for {}\", expected_type);\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Array/Map to Invalid Scalar Conversions\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_array_to_scalar_invalid_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Array to scalar conversions that should always fail\n    ///\n    /// Comprehensive testing of array-to-scalar conversion failures.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Coverage\n    ///\n    /// - Empty array to scalar\n    /// - Single-element array to scalar (still invalid - must explicitly access)\n    /// - Multi-element array to scalar\n    /// - Nested array to scalar\n\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (r#\"items: []\"#, \"empty array\", \"integer\"),\n        (r#\"count: [42]\"#, \"single-element array\", \"integer\"),\n        (r#\"ports: [8000, 8001, 8002]\"#, \"multi-element array\", \"integer\"),\n        (r#\"matrix: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]\"#, \"nested array\", \"integer\"),\n        (r#\"flags: [true, false]\"#, \"boolean array\", \"boolean\"),\n        (r#\"rates: [1.5, 2.5, 3.5]\"#, \"float array\", \"float\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n        assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\", description);\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        // Get the first key-value pair\n        let key = value.as_mapping().unwrap().keys().next().unwrap().as_str().unwrap();\n        let array_value = &value[key];\n\n        assert!(array_value.is_sequence(), \"Value should be array for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify conversion fails\n        let result = match expected_type {\n            \"integer\" => array_value.as_i64().is_some(),\n            \"boolean\" => array_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n            \"float\" => array_value.as_f64().is_some(),\n            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n        };\n\n        assert!(!result,\n            \"Array should not convert to {} for {}\", expected_type, description);\n\n        // Verify error handling\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(key, expected_type, \"array\");\n        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for array to {} ({})\", expected_type, description);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_map_to_scalar_invalid_conversions() {\n    /// Test: Map (object) to scalar conversions that should always fail\n map\", \"integer\"),\n        (r#\"server: {host: localhost, port: 8080}\"#, \"multi-key map\", \"string\"),\n        (r#\"database: {host: localhost, port: 5432, name: test}\"#, \"multi-field map\", \"boolean\"),\n        (r#\"nested: {outer: {inner: 42}}\"#, \"nested map\", \"integer\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (yaml, description, expected_type) in test_cases {\n        let value: Result<Value, _> =      \"boolean\" => map_value.as_bool().is_some(),\n            _ => panic!(\"Unexpected expected type: {}\", expected_type),\n        };\n\n        assert!(!result,\n            \"Map should not convert to {} for {}\", expected_type, description);\n\n        // Verify error handling\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(key, expected_type, \"map\");\n test_array_to_map_invalid_conversion() {\n    /// Test: Array to map conversion should fail\n    ///\n    /// Arrays cannot be treated as maps/objects - this is a structural mismatch.\n\n    let &value[\"servers\"];\n\n    assert!(servers_value.is_sequence(), \"Servers should be an array\");\n\n    // Verify array cannot be treated as mapping\n
